message:
feat(core,abi): port RFC 3492 Punycode to core::idna::punycode (bd-pn-epic)

Lifts ~170 lines of RFC 3492 Punycode logic out of glibc_internal_abi.rs
(7 constants + adapt + 2 digit helpers + encode (~67 lines) + decode
(~52 lines)) into a pure-safe frankenlibc_core::idna::punycode module.
Used by __idna_to_dns_encoding / __idna_from_dns_encoding for IDNA
hostname conversion.

New core surface:
  pub const BASE: u32 = 36;
  pub const TMIN: u32 = 1;
  pub const TMAX: u32 = 26;
  pub const SKEW: u32 = 38;
  pub const DAMP: u32 = 700;
  pub const INITIAL_BIAS: u32 = 72;
  pub const INITIAL_N: u32 = 128;
  pub fn encode(input: &[u32]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>;
  pub fn decode(input: &[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u32>>;

Defensive overflow handling via checked_add/checked_mul throughout
(matching the previous abi impl).

abi changes:
  - Drop the 7 PUNYCODE_* constants + 5 inline functions.
  - `use frankenlibc_core::idna::punycode::{decode as punycode_decode,
    encode as punycode_encode};` keeps the 2 call sites in
    __idna_to_dns_encoding / __idna_from_dns_encoding byte-identical.

Test coverage: 25 unit tests including all 11 RFC 3492 §7.1
reference vectors (Arabic/Chinese-simp/Chinese-trad/Czech/Hebrew/
Hindi/Japanese/Korean/Russian/Spanish/Vietnamese), digit helper
case-folding + alnum-only acceptance, the RFC §6.2 quirk where a
no-delimiter input is treated as variable-section, malformed-input
rejection (non-ASCII in basic section, invalid base-36 digit,
truncated extended section), and empty-input edge cases.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-pn-2):
  - 25 new core::idna::punycode tests pass (incl. all 11 RFC vectors)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 12/12 idna integration tests pass (incl. Japanese + Chinese
    + Unicode round-trips through the C ABI)
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-pn-epic.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+//! IDNA (Internationalized Domain Names in Applications) primitives.
+//!
+//! Pure-safe Rust implementations of the byte-level building blocks
+//! used by `__idna_to_dns_encoding` / `__idna_from_dns_encoding` in
+//! the abi layer. Today this is just RFC 3492 Punycode; future work
+//! could add the IDNA 2008 mapping tables (UTS #46) here too.
+
+pub mod punycode;
